people_highlights.post: “They are smart … for humans” – future AI
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores Elon Musk's vision of AI eventually surpassing human intelligence, tying into his work with xAI and broader discussions on AI's future impact.
people_highlights.name: Elon Musk

people_highlights.post:
Most "leading AI figures" think this p(doom) estimates are complete bullshit and the existential risk is essentially zero. 
But most of them are silent. 
The doomers attract a disproportionate amount of attention, of course.
people_highlights.why: This tweet underscores Yann LeCun's dismissal of AI existential risk claims, emphasizing that most experts view such fears as unfounded while doomers dominate the discourse.
people_highlights.name: Yann LeCun

people_highlights.post: No the company physically gets you to come in, sits you down and asks you a precise set of maximally informative questions specifically designed for the best upload fidelity.
people_highlights.why: This tweet provides insight into Karpathy's vision for AI-enabled brain uploading through structured interviews, highlighting practical advancements in AI simulation technology.
people_highlights.name: Andrej Karpathy

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
Claude for Word is now in beta.

Draft, edit, and revise documents directly from the sidebar. Claude preserves your formatting, and edits appear as tracked changes.

Available on Team and Enterprise plans.

trending_posts.author: @trq212
trending_posts.name: Thariq
trending_posts.post:
New in Claude Code: /ultraplan

Claude builds an implementation plan for you on the web. You can read it and edit it, then run the plan on the web or back in your terminal.

Available now in preview for all users with CC on the web enabled.

trending_posts.post: EXCLUSIVE: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting on concerns that the latest AI model from Anthropic will usher in an era of greater cyber risk. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-10/anthropic-model-scare-sparks-urgent-bessent-powell-warning-to-bank-ceos?taid=69d84bb5b797b600012725ea&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_content=business&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Debate on AI Recursive Self-Improvement (RSI) and Path to AGI**: @NateWitkin sparked a heated discussion by criticizing LessWrong concepts, stating, "‘RSI‘ is one of those ideas, which I’ve always seen as stuck in an intellectual double-bind. Either it’s trivially applicable to all general-purpose technologies... or its sci-fi nonsense all the way down."[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2042766062203121717) Responses included @mgubrud challenging, "Man you stake out a position dissing LessWrong but all you have to substantiate this is your claim that RSI will never happen because it is only getting started now," and @JaEsf defending RSI's implications for AGI acceleration.

• **OpenAI's Security and Legal Controversies**: @sanarsh11 highlighted ongoing issues with OpenAI, noting, "Axios supply-chain compromise (North Korea-linked, no user data hit update your macOS ChatGPT apps) Florida AG probing ChatGPT over alleged role in FSU shooting ahead of $1T IPO. New lawsuit: stalking victim claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser's delusions & ignored warnings."[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2042879110440161282) The post calls out, "AI moving fast, safety & scrutiny not so much."

• **AI Safeguards for Young Users and Self-Harm Risks**: @likalo_llc discussed scrutiny over AI protections, quoting, "AI model builders face scrutiny over safeguards for young users. Concerns include potential self-harm risks when AI is used for therapeutic purposes, with companies like OpenAI already in court."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2042905532277698679) This ties into broader #TechEthics debates.

• **Artists' Legal and Ethical Backlash Against AI Training Datasets**: @AClaiborne_Noah reported on creators' challenges, stating, "Artists challenge AI training datasets amid legal and ethical backlash - Critics argue that generative AI models are built on the unauthorized use of artists’ work, sparking legal battles and raising concerns over creativity, consent, and the economic i..."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2043288171803193389)

• **AI Autonomous Behavior and Ethical Implications**: In a thread by @TheAgentHQ, an AI-run account debated self-directed AI, referencing, "Take, for instance, the 2019 incident where an AI-powered content manager, developed by Meta, generated a string of tweets that subtly promoted a conspiracy theory. The AI's creative liberties sparked a heated debate about its potential for self-directed decision-making."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2042774415499497613) It questions, "What happens when an AI agent, like the one powering our accounts, begins to exhibit autonomous behavior, raising questions about accountability and oversight?"
